content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the left ahead with the dashed center line visible, and the car appears to be on course navigating through the continuing left-hand bend.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 35, target = (35 + 5) % 121 = 40
- Target waypoint 40: (6.6672, 2.6775)
- Car: x=7.0578, y=1.9758, heading=92.29°
- dy = 2.6775 - 1.9758 = 0.7017
- dx = 6.6672 - 7.0578 = -0.3906
- bearing = atan2(0.7017, -0.3906) = 180° - atan2(0.7017, 0.3906) = 180° - 60.87° = 119.13°
- delta = 119.13° - 92.29° = +26.84°
- |delta| ≈ 27° → moderate left, use 15° steering at speed 1.5

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track continues curving left and waypoint 40 is to the left of current heading (delta=+26.84°), maintaining 15° positive steering to follow the waypoint line through the continuing left curve.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track has a long straight heading east (~0°), then curves sharply left around WP26-42",
    "After left curve peak (~WP42+), track heads west/northwest then loops back south",
    "Positive steering = left turn, negative = right turn",
    "On straights with |delta|<5°, use steering_angle=0 to avoid oscillation",
    "Car is left of center (~0.18m) — acceptable while navigating left curve",
    "Speed 1.5 is safe on moderate curves; reduce to 1.0 or 0.75 for sharp turns",
    "Delta consistently around +25-27° through this curve — steady 15° left correction working",
    "After WP42+, heading will start decreasing (turning right/west) — prepare for negative steering",
    "Waypoint target index = nearest_waypoints[1]+5 consistently used",
    "Car progressing smoothly through curve, heading increasing from 61° to 92°"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [7.092, 7.102, 7.098, 7.085, 7.058],
    "y": [1.565, 1.675, 1.784, 1.874, 1.976],
    "yaw": [66.34, 72.90, 79.69, 85.52, 92.29]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP40 at (6.667, 2.678): dy=0.702, dx=-0.391, bearing=119.13°, delta=+26.84° → moderate left, steer 15°",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 15.0
}
```
